kill_fact: The catalyst date in the dossier is STALE/WRONG. The file asserts a board-approved spin 'targeted mid-to-late 2026' and treats the catalyst as in-window. On the Q1 FY2026 earnings call (05/05/2026) CEO Stuart Bradie stated verbatim: 'we are working toward an effective spin date of January 4, 2027' — a ~6-month SLIP out of the assumed 2026 window, to the first business day of fiscal 2027. Worse, the Form 10 is STILL CONFIDENTIAL: management 'anticipate[s] a public filing in September' 2026, so the gating regulatory step (public Form 10 + effectiveness) has not even begun publicly as of 06/22/2026. The spread has NOT closed (stock still $32.62, at/near its 52-wk low $29.94) and the going-concern floor IS real (Q1 net income $102M, EPS $0.80, $0.66 div, FCF+), so the asymmetry survives — but the 'PENDING_HARD / in-window' framing and the 'mid-to-late 2026' milestone are false. This is a 2027 event with the first public milestone ~3 months out, NOT an imminent in-window fire; carry-cost and further-slip risk are higher than the file represents.
corrections: 1) dated_milestone / catalyst_summary / forcing_function: replace 'mid-to-late 2026' with effective spin date TARGETED January 4, 2027 (first business day of FY2027), per CEO on 05/05/2026 Q1 call. 2) Form 10 is CONFIDENTIAL, not merely 'not yet effective' — public filing only anticipated ~September 2026, so the SEC-effectiveness forcing function is months away. 3) Status should read as a board-approved 2027-dated spin with no public regulatory milestone yet, NOT an in-window PENDING_HARD setup. 4) Live MTS softness is real: Q1 FY2026 revenue $1.923B (down 5%), net income $102M (down), supporting the street's $78->$65 cut and a possible weaker SpinCo launch multiple than the 11-13x assumed. SoP magnitude and the going-concern floor (~$33/spot, $29.94 hard support) are NOT refuted; the timing/imminence is.
evidence: KBR Q1 FY2026 earnings call transcript, 05/05/2026 — CEO Stuart Bradie: 'we are working toward an effective spin date of January 4, 2027, so the first business day of fiscal '27'; and on Form 10: 'We have confidentially resubmitted our Form 10 ... before transitioning to a public filing, which we currently anticipate in September' (fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/05/kbr-kbr-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript/).

KBR Q1 FY2026 results (reported 05/05/2026): revenue $1.923B (down ~5%), operating income $180M (down ~11%), net income attributable to KBR $102M, diluted EPS $0.80 — MTS govt softness confirmed (stocktitan.net KBR 10-Q / Q1 fiscal 2026 release).

KBR 8-K, exhibit 99.1 dated 09/24/2025 (SEC EDGAR CIK 0001357615) — board unanimously approved strategic intent to spin off Mission Technology Solutions, tax-free, subject to Form 10 effectiveness and IRS opinion; original target 'mid-to-late 2026' (sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001357615/000135761525000053/exhibit99-109242025.htm).

Market data 06/22/2026 (stockanalysis.com/stocks/kbr/): price $32.62, mkt cap ~$4.14B, fwd P/E ~7.95, 52-wk range $29.94-$53.50 — spread still open, stock at trough multiple; consensus 12-mo target ~$46.57 (Buy).
